reviewText: Wow! I've never cracked open an appliance to fix it before so I was apprehensive about this. When I read how easy it was and thought about the savings, I had to try. The door of my Amana ADB1500AWS had been sticking for a while. This week it finally stuck shut for good, and I had to use a small flathead screwdriver slid in under the handle in order to force the latch by pushing from the outside. Then it was broken for real. Well, buying this latch assembly was worth it-- it was a total success! Here are the steps I took... 1. I got my supplies together. I ordered this and DEWALT DW2067 Torx Insert Bit Set, 7-Piece for my ratcheting screwdriver. I ordered them using 2-day shipping, but I actually received the stuff in just one day. 2. With the T20 torx bit in my ratcheting screwdriver, I opened the dishwasher door and removed all 11 torx screws from the inside perimeter of the door, including the top center two that held the latch assembly in place. I washed the screws and the inside of the door under the screw locations because they were dusty, and then I removed the door's inside plastic panel and set it to the side. The inner door panel was connected by wires-- I didn't disconnect them, so the inner panel was attached the whole time even though the panel was set to the side. 3. The old latch assembly had only been attached by the door's two center screws that I'd already removed and two pairs of wire plugs. Without tools, I pulled out the latch assembly including the door handle and disconnected the wire plugs. 4. I plugged in the new latch assembly the way the previous one had been connected, and dropped it into its place, handle first. 5. I returned the inner door to its correct spot and put the screws back in tightly. 6. I closed the dishwasher door and appreciated the assured "click" and the door's satisfying ability to open by a smooth, easy motion of the handle. I closed the door and began a cycle to make sure it would start. I opened the door after the cycle started to make sure the dishwasher would pause. Everything worked perfectly. This was a big win for me-- a money saver, a convenient-easy-practical fix, and confidence-builder, too! It would have probably taken more time to even get a repair-person out here for the initial visit!

reviewText:
With the instructions from a youtube video, straight forward fix for a top load washer that wouldn't spin or agitate.  Ordered a couple just to have an extra on-hand.  Hardest part was repositioning the washer's case back on the frame, aligning all the tabs.

If your washer will fill and drain, but won't spin or agitate, google for "washer won't spin or agitate" for how-to-do youtube video's, order this part, and do it yourself; you wallet will be grateful.

Downside:  before, washer would produce a LOUD "clunk" as it switched cycles, allowing me to track its progress.  Now, no "clunk"; runs quiet.

reviewText:
Not sure about longevity but I just put this in yesterday with no problems.  Previous pump in my Kenmore Elite 11027087603 (sometimes listed as 110.27087603) developed a crack in the pump housing so was leaking a few ounces each time it ran.  Tried the JB Weld Water Weld epoxy repair on top of the Lazer Bond and it slowed the leak but still dripped so I just ordered this pump.  Interesting too was that the BB and marble rattle sound I had in the washer for the last several years was fixed as well... turns out there were two pennies in the drain pump!

To replace it, I read where people had somebody help and tilt the washer back.  I just took the back off and put the washer on a furniture dolly with wheels and that raised it high enough for me to easily take the 3 screws out to replace it.

Did a load last night and no leaks and it drained just fine so all is good so far!

reviewText: My 25 year old Kenmore Dryer just bit the dust, and as long as I was going to go through this trouble and inconvenience I just decided to buy a new washer as well.  I actually went to "Famous Brand  Home Supply" store and paid for a new washer and dryer; fortunately for me they were running 1 week behind on delivery so I had plenty of time to more research and change my mind!!  Even the very expensive HE washers have way too many 1 star reviews and I had a sick feeling in my stomach that I could just as well have taken that money to a local casino.  A friend insisted that I go to a local appliance store known for their good quality appliances and good prices.  I did, and when the salesman showed me the Speed Queen AWN432 I fell in love at first sight!!  It was exactly what I was looking for; a tried and true quality brand, a top loader with an agitator that cleans clothes and does not tear them up, a machine that does not rely on a computer to do it's job.  It is still new and I cannot comment on long term quality but I feel very confident after having read many reviews since purchase.  The capacity is not huge but it is enough for myself and my daughter.  I hope to be able to wash blankets and sheets in it but will still have to take queen size comforters to a commercial laundry or dry cleaner.  The spin cycle is very similar to my old Kenmore and that's just fine with me.  I love the fact that it has a second wash cycle (which I probably won't need much) and a soak cycle.  The 3 year warranty alone adds a lot of value; the cheapest price I saw for this as an "add on" was around $70.  The cost of this washer was probably very similar to my old washer with inflation factored in and I got more use out of that one than I had any right to expect!  If you are like me and leery of the new HE washers and I recommend this one!

reviewText:
I was looking to replace the agitator dogs for my late model (late 1980's)Kenmore washer. The tool that was available to check if these particular dogs would fit my washer came in very handy, as there was no guessing and hoping they were the correct size.
The small package containing the 4 dogs came in a factory wrapped package, and I received it about 3 days after I placed the order.  They are very sturdy and excellent quailty. My washer is working like it is brand new again.
I was told by a major appliance store, it would cost $40 for them to come to the house and "check" the machine,  then parts and labor would be extra. They quoted me $8.99 for the dogs, plus $5.99 shipping. After watching a few videos on the internet,  I realized just how easy this job is to DIY.
Using this hi-quality product,  I had the job done for just the low cost of this product!!
Your agitator dogs have "teeth" on them which allow your top agitator to spin properly on your machine. The "teeth" were worn down to nothing,  causing my agitator to not spin. If you are having this problem,  order this product,  watch a few videos,  and save yourself a TON of money by fixing it yourself.  I did and my clothes are much cleaner than before!
Definately recommended! !

reviewText:
It's a daunting prospect to order a large item like this, sight unseen, from a seller you've never heard of.  Well, it turned out spectacularly!  Delivery was fast and the shipping company helped me inspect it before I signed for it (which was an absolute requirement of the seller).  It sat in my entryway for about a month before I could get my brother-in-law over to help me install it.  Also, I had aluminum wiring to the oven (not the '60s era aluminum branch wiring) which is more common than you may think... so I had to research the correct way to attach the copper leads to the aluminum (NOT wire nuts - actual Cu to Al junction blocks insulated with "splicing" tape and electrical tape - check codes if you encounter this). The wiring conduit was not very long and would have made for a difficult install, so I removed the conduit from the oven I was replacing and put it on this oven - doubling the length (Fridigaire really should add a couple more feet to their conduit).

My oven cutout appeared to be the perfect size for this oven until I realized a showstopper - the existing electrical box was mounted at the back of the cutout, but took up 1 1/2" of depth that this oven would need to slide all the way in.  I had to remount the box to the top of the opening (behind the much shallower microwave part) and I was fortunate that there was enough house wiring there to allow this to happen... it was close!

This is a big, heavy unit (about 250 lbs.) and I believe that during shipping, the microwave part twisted slightly relative to the oven - the top unit was about 1/2" out of line with the bottom.  Physical wrenching wouldn't work, so I had to remove the microwave and actually drill out the metal attachments to get the units to line up properly.  A bit of extra work, but probably expected for a job like this.

Once lifted into the cutout, it slid back easily and was secured with just two screws!  And it looks great!

And the best part... I love my new oven and microwave!  The unit it replaced was a GE from 1986, so the microwave is FAR more powerful and the digitally-controlled oven has features (like probe cooking) that I now can't live without.  When a recipe says "heat to an internal temperature of 165 degrees", I immediately pull out the probe and let 'er go!  Tell the oven to bake until the probe says it's 165, and then the oven goes into a "keep warm" mode until you get your perfectly-cooked food out.  Man, I didn't know what I was missing!  Really love this.  And there's all the other things this features, like convection cooking and self cleaning.  And a nice, LOUD alarm on the oven.

Then they go and put the most feeble buzzer imaginable on the microwave... so low that I can't hear it (although a woman friend could - barely).  Don't know why they couldn't have used the same buzzer that was on the oven, but they didn't.  BUT the microwave is so powerful and cooks so evenly, that I'll give Frigidaire a pass on that.  But FIX IT, folks.

Very pleased with my purchase and the product is amazing.  One of the best larger purchases I've made this year!
summary: Wonderful oven and microwave combo.  Great purchase!

reviewText: I have a Kenmore HE4. Had the SUDS message on my display. This pump does work. However, the more wash cycles we did, the noise of the pump increased. It was already very loud when first installed, but got louder and louder the more cycles we did. I decided to take the pump off and have a look. Well, water had gotten past the impeller shaft and had filled the pump with water. I don't think that is where water is supposed to be. This is not an oem part and would recommend spending the $150 for a genuine part, even though it comes with the other parts you don't need. I bought the OEM part, installed it, and now the washer is just as quiet as it was when new.  One thing to note, if you have SUDS on your display, make sure there is nothing stuck in the drain (the one behind the bottom panel). I didn't think there was cause I opened the clean out plug and didn't see anything, BUT make sure to take the black rubber drain off the back of the clean out plug housing to look in there. I found part of a bra stuck in there which may have been causing the Suds message all along .
summary: Don't recommend, get OEM instead and you can't go wrong. Learned hard lesson once again.

This is (OK, besides maybe my tv) my favorite possession!  Don't even laugh, I don't have a W/D and it is amazing.  Easy to use, works in 5 min -  I wish I had gotten one ages ago. And now with winter and no porch time for clothes drying, and no desire to schlep laundry in the snow, I  love it even more.

THANK YOU.

See my review for the Whirlpool 279311 Igniter. It has pictures too.

I replaced these 2 coils when I had the dryer opened up to replace the igniter. They are on the gas valve/control unit in the front lower left of the dryer. They are held on with a metal top bracket. I unplugged the plastic wire clip from the 2 old ones, removed the metal bracket, put the new ones in, replaced the bracket, and reconnected the wire clips. I don't know if they were bad, but since I was replacing the igniter I figured I'd replace these too, only about 10 bucks and the dryer is already opened up. Now it works great again.

reviewText: My freezer portion of my refrigerator/freezer kept freezing up in one solid piece of ice.  I replaced the heater element (product number WR51X1005) and the thermostat (this product) for less than $50 total and the problem is solved.  I was expecting aa service repair person to charge me nearly $1,000 had I used them instead.  Try this first; it's easy, effective, and if your coils are freezing up; this is likely your problem.  Tip: Remove the old one, use a continuity tester.  If the heater is not continuous, then it needs to be replaced.  Simple.

reviewText: I ordered this Avanti compact refrigerator (AR4596SS) on Wednesday, May 7, 2014. Since I have Amazon Prime, I expected it to arrive on Friday. And Friday morning, the tracking on Amazon said it was "Out for Delivery." I needed to take it up a flight of stairs, so I had a helper standing by to help me get it into my home. By 6pm on Friday it had not arrived. So I called Amazon. After waiting on hold for several minutes, I was told it was on its way and would arrive in an hour. Well, 7pm came and went, and it still did not arrive. So I called Amazon again. This time I was told that it was never even scheduled for delivery on Friday; that it would be coming on Saturday. So I asked, "Why did the Web site say it was 'Our for delivery' and why was I was told at 6pm that it was coming in an hour?" All the Amazon representative could say was that that was the info the site administrator and the previous rep had, but she now had the correct info. If it had just been me I wouldn't have been too upset, but as I said, I had another person waiting to help me. So we both wasted the whole day waiting for a package that wasn't even scheduled to arrive until the next day. In addition, before I ordered it, I checked the weather, as I knew I'd be outside for a while hooking the fridge up to a dolly. Friday the weather was to be nice, but storms were forecast for Saturday. That is why I ordered it on Wednesday, so I'd get it on Friday. I didn't want to be outside on Saturday in the middle of a lightening storm messing with a metal fridge and dolly. The forecast was right: it was nice on Friday and stormed on Saturday. But the fridge did not come until 5pm on Saturday, so the storms had passed by then. But it was two wasted days for me and my helper. However, for all of the hassles, Amazon refunded a significant portion of the cost. So for that I am thankful, and it is why Amazon is my "go-to" place for Internet shopping. Their service is usually very good, and if they do screw something up, they usually make it good somehow. But be forewarned when ordering large items, as there could be hassles with the shipping. That said, on the fridge itself, the reason I was getting a compact fridge was for "overflow" from my main fridge. Specifically, I try to eat mostly organic food, especially fruits and veggies. The best place to get such in the Pittsburgh area where I live is Pittsburgh's "East End Food Co-op." But that is over an half hour drive for me, so I only go once a month. As such, I really need to stock-up when I go. A compact fridge enables me to get even more fresh produce and not have it spoil by having it in a fridge the whole time. I had a compact fridge before this one, but it had a freezer compartment. As soon s I got that fridge, I knew it was a mistake. The freezer compartment was worthless. The only thing it would freeze was water. Even spaghetti sauce or chicken juice did not freeze. But what it did do was get covered with ice very quickly, so I had to defrost the freezer every month or two. Doing so required taking everything out of the fridge and freezer compartment, putting a tray under the freezer compartment to catch the melting ice, then wiping up the water that overflowed the tray, and then putting everything back in. It was a real pain, so I got rid of it. Unless you have a pressing need to make ice, do not get a compact fridge with a freezer compartment. That is why I got this Avanti fridge, as it does not have a freezer compartment. In any case, I finally got the fridge into my home and set up where I wanted it. The front feet are adjustable, so I adjusted them as needed. But I read somewhere in researching compact fridges that you should let a fridge sit for several hours after moving it before plugging it in. I'm not exactly sure why, but it has something to do with letting the cooling elements settle. So not wanting to damage my new fridge, I let it sit overnight. The next morning, I plugged it in and put a fridge thermometer in it. But when I went to turn it on and adjust the temperature, I noticed a minor problem with this fridge. The inside is white, including the knob for adjusting the temperature (located on the right hand side in about the middle of the fridge), but the numbering on the knob is light gold, so it is barely readable. I had to use a magnifying glass to read the numbers. But with doing so, I set it to the middle and let it cool. It only took about an hour. And I've only had to adjust the temperature a couple of times since then, so the hard to read numbering on the knob is not too much of a problem. But the second problem is much more significant. After I got everything in it with no problems, I put a two quart jug of reconstituted protein powder on the door and shut it. But when I went to open the door later, it was stuck! I couldn't open it. I had to shake it to get it opened, which with the "not moving it after it is plugged in advice" was probably not a good idea, but it was the only way I could get it opened. After some investigation I figured out what happened. The jug was "so heavy" it bent the door down, so it was catching on the bottom of the fridge. I had to pull up rather strongly on the door to straiten it up, which made me uncomfortable, as I was afraid I would break it. I didn't, but I knew then that I could not put anything "heavy" on the door. But even with only putting a few very light items on the door, the door still sticks. So I need to gently pull up on the door each and every time I open and close it. Since there is no handle on the door, this is not easy to do. I have to use both hands and grab onto the little grip at the top of the door with my fingertips to open and close it. This is a real pain, especially when I am putting something into the fridge. Rather than holding the item in hand and opening the fridge with the other as anyone reading this has probably done thousands of times without thinking about it, I have to set the item down, open the door with both hands, then hold the door open with one hand as I pick up the item with the other hand and then put it into the fridge. Because this is something that irritates me every time I open and close the fridge, I am only giving it three stars. But that is shame, as if it wasn't for that one problem, I would probably give this fridge five stars. As indicated, I got this fridge in May; it is now October. So I've had it for five months, and it has been working fine that whole time. The fridge stays cool, even when I load it up after my monthly Co-op trip, so it is a reliable fridge. Plus, there is a lot of space for a compact fridge, so I can really stock up on healthy foods. And eating lots of organic produce and other healthy foods has improved my health. That is no surprise, as I discuss the benefits of doing so in my God-given Foods Eating Plan book. But when I was between compact fridges, I wasn't going to the Co-op, so I wasn't eating quite as healthy as I am now. Bottom line: this Avanti fridge is reliable and roomy, and the lack of a freezer compartment is a big plus. If they would just fix the door and use black numbers on the temperature knob, it would be ideal.

reviewText:
We purchased an Electrolux EIMGD60LT gas dryer which was installed 6 days ago.  It has stopped getting hot and turns off with an E61 error code.  Now we are stuck with two wet loads of laundry and no way to dry them.  Not what you expect after paying $1200.00 for a new dryer. I'll be calling tomorrow for warranty service and letting the dealer know. This is our first Electrolux, appliance but may be the last.

I thought the Samsung it replaced was junk but at least that $1200.00 dryer lasted 6-7 years before I got tired of repairing it.

Update: We had the machine repaired under warranty (no cost to us) which took about two weeks.  Since then, it has performed well with no further problems.

reviewText: We rather nervously ordered this stove from Amazon. We had never cooked on induction before, had never ordered something so expensive from Amazon before.  We thought our worst fears were realized when the stove was delivered and the box it was in  was practically destroyed.  There were holes in the side, the Styrofoam was smashed.  I am giving this 4 instead of 5 stars  for the packaging  that really wasn't study enough.  We had an existing electric cooktop.  It was very easy to pull out our old stove and swap this new one in. I was surprised  just how easy it was.  We have been cooking on the stove for 3 days and we LOVE it.  It heats pots with high iron content  in just a few seconds. We amaze all of our visitors with how quickly it brings water to a boil.  Adjusting the temperature up or down has near instant results on the cooking temperature. This is such a a huge improvement over the traditional radiant electric stove we were using.  One note on pots-  the stove is a bit fussy.  We bought a new frying pan  that claimed to be "induction ready".  Its iron content was so low that the stove made weird noises,  with the conductors turning on and off in protest.  Switching to  pots with higher iron content  solved the problem. The stove is easy to clean  and cools down quickly.  Cooking is once again a delight,

reviewText:
What I like:
This machine does a good job of getting my laundry clean and can also handle queen sheets and bath towels (I wash the fitted and flat sheets seperately. For towels, 2 bath towels + 2 hand towels at a time). I wash laundry for 1 on average 4 times a week so this purchase paid for itself right away. I don't need to go to a laundry, I can wash whenever I want and I avoid using the nasty, expensive laundromat machines. I can be at home, also I save on gas by not driving.
I have been using this machine heavily for several months now and the motors are still as strong as when they were new.

The not so good:
My machine leaks from the top edge a little bit when running the spin dryer. I just put a towel underneath to absorb drips. It's not very much and I usually cause an equal amount just moving the wet stuff into the spinner.
The agitator will come off if I don't tighten the screw that holds it on every few loads. This happened the first week I used it and it's been a minor routine maintenance issue from the start.
The drain pump is flakey. When rinsing clothes in the spinner the pump is inconsistent in draining accumulated water, sometimes it kicks in, other times it doesn't and the spinner gets too full of water to spin properly (if I turn off the spinner for a second then turn it back on the pump will activate and drain the water). This means I need to babysit when rinsing this way so the rinse tub doesn't overfill. It seems that you cannot drain the wash tub and rinse with the spinner simultaneously as well. It is important that the drain hose be elevated for the pump to work properly btw. There is a bit of strategy involved if you're washing more than one load.
The fill hose doesn't come with any fittings that would work on any plumbing I've seen here in the USA.
After I got some fittings and washers it works very well and fills the tub quickly. See the photos I'm sharing to see the parts I used and how I set it up. There are many different types and sizes of adapters you can use, it depends on the fawcett size and what is available where you buy your parts. I used a GH (garden hose) adapter from a barb kit and an o-ring to secure the flange that came with the fill hose.

Overall I'm a bit disappointed with the dripping, the lack of consistency of the pump, and the agitator frequently coming loose. I chose this purchase over a similar but more popular and higher rated brand that was backordered for over 30 days and I couldn't wait that long! There was also a significant price difference with my purchase being the cheaper one.
Still, I'm happy with this purchase. From what I've read in these reviews most people are not having the same problems with their washers. If It weren't for these issues this product would be 5 stars.

reviewText:
We have looked at induction cook tops for years, and finally our old range died, so he opportunity came to buy a new one.  This is a really well designed range, with a number of safety features that build confidence.  The speed to heat the pans, and to stop heating pans when power is switched off, is very like gas, but of course, unlike gas, the surface is flat and uncluttered, so very easy to clean.  The oven work really well, large capacity, with several modes of operation.

If I had to criticize the design, it would be for those of us whose eyes are not what they were.  The main cooktop controls are on the surface and in relatively low contrast, making it less than obvious what and how the controls work.  After a few uses, it becomes easy and instinctive, so not enough of a problem to impact my assessment.

Overall, so far it has been a pleasure to have such  a great new range.

reviewText:
I purchased my matching washer and dryer at sears and have this exact model.  First I have to say this was not my first choice as I tend to want to purchase the "cadillac" of every home appliance.  However financially I had to be slightly more modest with my selection.  I am amazed with this machine and pleasantly suprised!!

-It is EXTREMELY QUIET and the loudest part is at the end when it is spinning out the water, it kind of sounds like a quietly-muffled jet engine..lol

-On the very first wash I did my CLOTHES CAME OUT 3 TIMES BRIGHTER than my last regular top loader.

-Oh!! And it BARELY USES WATER....It turns to the right then the left coating the clothes with the detergent water until it saturates them completely.  Then it begins the washing process where it turns several times to the right and then to the left all the while it basically beats the clothes against the drum as it turns..Kind of reminds me of the old times when people would wash their clothes in the creek and beat them against the rocks. :)  It is such a simple concept and yet it tooks so long to get here!

-The other thing I like is the detergent compartment...it has 3 places to put your detergent, bleach and softener and then you walk away, no going back to add the softener.

-Another thing I noticed is that when the spinning process is complete the drum turns a few more times to the right and left to loosen the clothes from the sides...they come out of the washer fluffed and easy to keep wrinkle-free.

I haven't found anything yet that I don't like about this washer....I am a hard person to please and I am tickled pink with this!  :)

Expected firm, solid hi-temperature strips -- these are flexible and tacky-feeling to the touch ... not what I expected, but they work well.

The only problem with these, is when you happen to come in contact with these with your hand, they roll-over and you have to put them back in place, which is aggravating, as it seems to happen way too often -- because they have such a "tacky texture" -- we really didn't plan to re-learn how to physically work in our own kitchen to use this product, but that's what we've had to do.  If I were to replace these, I'd sure look for the ones that DO NOT HAVE THE TACKY FEEL that these have, which catches and causes the product to roll-over on the counter or stove

If I were to replace these, I'd sure look for the ones that DO NOT HAVE THE TACKY FEEL that these have, which catches and causes the product to roll-over on the counter or stove

reviewText:
I have seen the terrible reviews for this refrigerator model in black and in the stainless.  I had issues initially with the freezer acting like an old non defrost freezer and getting coated with ice.  I called for service and the guy who came out said the rails that the door rolls on and the seal needed to be replaced.  After doing this, the freezer has worked perfectly with no ice/frost buildup, the ice maker makes the appropriate amount of ice which is far faster than taking two days to fill the small bucket.  I love the separate drawers where I can keep deli meats and fruit so we don't have to open the main fridge compartment to get to them.

If you are having freezing issues, suggest this solution.  It has remedied the slow ice production in the ice compartment, the freezing over so ice forms in the freezer and the frost on our food.  Good luck and I would highly recommend this.

I was so excited to have a dish washer in my apartment. This unit wouldn't fit under my cabinets, so I bought a cart from Lowes to put it on.

Pros:
Easy to use. Hose attaches to the faucet quickly
Dishes cleaned in no time - I would recommend rinsing baked on crud before using
Quiet - can use in the kitchen and not hear it in the living room

Cons:
It's heavy
The door can be difficult to open
Not sure if my rental apt sink was crap or there was a washer issue, water spurted everywhere when I used it.
Storing the hoses when not in use is diffuclt. They just hang off the back and take up space.

Overall, I would not recommend this product. The hassle of using it outweighs any benefit of not having to do the dishes.

reviewText:
+ all in one
+ energy efficient (low electric and water usage)
- spews out a ton of lint in drainage water that clogged my drainage pipe (over time) and had to pay a plumber $125 to unclog
- when using mesh filter to catch lint from water that goes into my utility sink to drain, sink can back up due to lint (see photos)
- end-to-end washing+drying time takes me about 3.5h :(

advice:
- don't dry jeans; wash jeans only in 'small load' mode, no drying; take jeans out immediately, flatten with hand and hang up immediately (eg. over shower rod)
- when drying regular loads, stop 20m-30m early to prevent from over-drying; slightly damp is better than over-dry
- use a filter in your utility sink to prevent lint from clogging your drain

reviewText:
My little boy is just tall enough to grab at the knobs on the stove, and has succeeded in breaking off two of them.

I figured that the complaints by the other reviewers were just manufacturing flukes, but if my experience is any guide, these flukes are all too common.

The some of the labels on the knob are melted and blurry, other labels disappeared within a few weeks after gentle cleanings.  Meanwhile, the knobs that came with the stove look quite similar but have retained their labels.

I think that GE should be ashamed to sell these at all; it sullies their name and the price suggests that the customer is receiving a lot more quality than they actually get.  I wouldn't want these to be in my kitchen at all, even if they cost $1.

My advice:  seek other sources; maybe a local appliance store where you can inspect the merchandise first.

Two stars because the knobs fit and work.

reviewText: Ordered a new "improved" Wash Plate since I was already ordering a Suspension Kit.  The Wash Plate seems to result in less twisting or tangling of items in our Whirlpool Cabrio Washer.  The only issue I had was that It was a really difficult to get the old Wash Plate out, I ended up having to use the Wash Drum like a reverse pile-driver, to coax the Wash Plate off.  What had happened is that there was a really hard dark colored "black" build up on the main shaft, which was affecting the wash drum's ability to "float", it took several hours of scraping and cleaning to restore the shaft.  If I had to do it again, I would order a shaft replacement kit.  After I installed this Wash Plate and a Whirlpool W10189077 Suspension kit, and my washer is back to like new performance.  Just watch out for sharp edges inside the washer, especially when replacing the suspension parts..

reviewText: After having F&P washer dryer for 18 years we finally needed to replace the old units and after 2 years I can say they are a real disappointment. They run long, the washer has a short agitator (the ones in Australia have conventional ones) so it gets out of balance constantly if you place have a heavy load to wash. The dryer had to be replaced after fighting with the US Customer Service office as the lock on the drum lid was so loud it sounded like something snapped. They keep saying it does make a noise, but the local store got the field rep involved and he had it replaced, this one is quiet but slow to dry. It's great to save energy but time is also important, washing 4-5 loads of cloths is an all day job with the AquaSmart W&D. reviewText: This was a highly recommended deep fryer, and the quality of construction, ease of use and ability to clean up quickly were all important considerations.  It is very carefully packaged, instructions are good, perhaps not great, but easy to follow if you take a moment to look at the pictures.  It is fast, clean and no one gets injured!  I had sort of hoped that the frying basin area might be larger in surface area (not necessarily deeper) so that I could do more items at a time, but for most foods, fries, etc., I can see that having them stacked works fine.  I just love doing tempura, and that cannot be stacked as successfully because the batter will get all gigantically encrusted -- must keep the tempura pieces separated by a bit of space.  Otherwise, you can dip and fry, remove items and dip and fry to your hearts content, with minimal mess and still have relatively healthy meals.  Great for fish, but toss that old oil if doing seafoods!  With veges you can recycle the oil and that saves a lot of money.  We buy the big 1/2 gallon of 50% olive oil, 50% Canola blend and that works perfectly.

reviewText:
I bought this fridge 3 months ago and am now at a point to review:

PROS:
- Attractive
- Main fridge is easy to access
- Food humidity is better than I've seen with other fridges
- Excellent lighting in both freezer and fridge
- Nice glass shelves that are easy to clean
- Lots of shelves in the main fridge compartment

CONS:
- The biggest negative for me is the noise.  I find the fridge quite loud.  Maytag advertises it as being quiet.
- The freezer compartment is small.  There are 2 slide out drawers, and they fill very quickly.

I am unable to determine if I'm saving electricity because I bought this in September and over the past couple of months I've been baking more for Christmas, and using more power than I normally would.  By June 2014 I should have a better idea about whether I'm saving money on power.

I don't use the ice maker in this fridge.  If you were to use the ice maker you would have even less room.  I would rather use the ice try for extra freezer storage as I mentioned above the freezer is small in this fridge.

I would only recommend this to a friend if that friend did not mind the fridge noise.  If the bedrooms and living room are far enough away from the fridge it would not be noticeable.  It only comes on periodically, but when it is on I really notice it in my quiet house.

15-Jan-2014 - Sometimes the fridge comes on and it's very quiet.  Other times it's noisy.  After some more detective work, I determined that it's the freezer compartment fan that makes most of the noise and since the freezer fan does not always come on, it explains why it's quiet when it's on sometimes and noisy other times.

03-Oct-2014 - After a year of living with this fridge I will say that my power consumption went down over the year and it was likely due to this fridge.  This fridge replaced the same size fridge that was 17 years old.  I find food in the freezer is not prone to freezer burn which was a problem with my old fridge.  After 3 weeks foods would be burned in the older fridge.  I'm not certain how Maytag remedied this situation.  Maybe it's because the freezer is on the bottom.  Heat rises.  If the freezer is at the bottom maybe it does not come on as much which reduced freezer burn.  The noise is still very bothersome.  I have never gotten used to the loudness of the fridge.

02-Apr-2015 - The fridge has developed a grinding noise whenever it shuts off.  It will come on and the fan has always been noisy, but now there's a terrible rattle-grind that lasts for about 2 seconds.  It's annoying and makes me wonder if it's wearing out already.  I do not think I would ever buy another Maytag product or Whirlpool/KitchenAid for that matter.  No more of this junk will darken the doorway of my house.

reviewText:
When the icemaker quit in our 22' GE side by side, I discovered that there wasn't any water in the tray. This led me to replace the water inlet valve assembly on the back. Still no ice production. Thinking the icemaker itself is faulty, I removed it and wanted to see what makes it tick. Figured I was going to buy a new one anyway, it didn't matter if I broke it.

The white front cover on mine just pulled right off, revealing the cube size adjustment screw and two white plastic gears, and the wiring schematic inside the cover. Three screws hold the plate that has the power unit, main shaft and two switches on the back. The wires are long enough to tip the plate out and access the switches. This unit has the shut-off bail, and the end of it protrudes through a small hole on the plate. (Don't lose the spring!) The switches I tested all seemed to work, and are timed & activated by the eccentric shaft.

I reassembled it (minus the cover), hung it loosely in place and plugged it in. Nothing happening. I think the cycle is started by the thermostat, when it decides the water is frozen. With no water it didn't start. With a screwdriver in the slot of the small drive gear, rotating slowly counter-clockwise, the motor started and went through it's cycle. It's now making ice again and is half full this morning. Woo-Hoo!

EDIT: Sadly, it has stopped working, again. It makes ice, but I'm thinking the thermostat isn't sensing the ice is done. Therefore, it doesn't send power to the motor to start the cycle for the mold heater and extraction. I'm bummed, and looking for a replacement.

reviewText:
I've had this machine since 2006, and I am in love with it.  I live in a rental in NYC in a building without any laundry room.  This means that prior to this machine, my husband and I had to gather our clothing, and walk 4 blocks to a public laundry mat.  Not only is that inconvenient, but you're sharing machines with absolute strangers is not that sanitary and this really hit home when we were doing laundry and a homeless man came to wash his clothing in the machine next to us.

First thing I have to say is, this machine is NOT for everyone.  If I had the option of having a regular side by side washer and dryer, I would not have purchased/liked this machine.  The machine is small enough to fit anywhere.  I have it set up between the sink and refrigerator.  Since it is small enough to fit just about anywhere, this means that your loads are going to be smaller as well.  Personally I don't have a problem with load size....instead of doing laundry once a week, I do laundry every other day or every two days during the week.

I think the first thing people need to understand about this machine is that it's a trial and error process.  When you first start using it you'll have problems getting a load done as sometimes the machine is "unbalanced" and cannot spin.  When that happened to me I used to stop the machine, and reload it to make sure that it was balanced (making sure to have some heavy items in the bottom, smaller items in between, a couple of more heavy items on the top).  A normal cycle takes 58 minutes....my clothing comes out super clean.  Sometimes when I am in a hurry I'll do the "speed wash" cycle, which only takes 30 minutes.

Sure laundry takes a little longer because it's an all in one unit, but I have NEVER had a load longer than 3 hours.  This weekend I washed a complete queen size sheet set (fitted sheet, 2 pillow cases, and a sheet) and it took 2:30 hours from start to finish.  Did it come out wrinkled???  Yes, but I personally don't care since it's my bedding.  Was it dried when I pulled it out....YES!!!  That I do care about.  I read some of these reviews and I am amazed at the reasons people give it low rating (can't wash another load while drying...really? you didn't figure that one out before you brought it?)  Is it a perfect machine...no, but for people without regular machine options I think it great.

Size of the loads....this is something else you have to play around with.  When I first started using the machine, I had read that you should be careful with your load size, so I was making my loads really small.  I just finished a load right now which included 8 shirts, 2 shorts, 1 long pajama pants, 3 underwear....the total time for this load, 2 hours (using speed wash, 30 minutes, and drying with timer set at 1:30 hour).  My load was clean and dry.  Taking your clothing out of the dryer is something else you have to get used to because the load will feel damp, but it's not.  All you have to do is shake the condensation out (and I really do mean shake.  What I do is when I pull it out of the machine, I shake it and set that piece of clothing on the back of the dining room chair.  By the time I am done pulling everything out, all the clothing is dry and ready to be folded and put away.

Vibrations/Shaking...the machine is fairly quiet.  The only thing that make a loud noise is when it's expelling water and even that is tolerable.  I can watch tv in the other room and it doesn't bother me.  When the machine spins, it spins very fast (which helps with making sure your clothing is almost dry by the time the wash is done) but this means that there will be some vibration.  My floor is wood, and you don't really hear it.  Now if I touch the counter or stove I can feel the vibration....but not hear it.  My downstairs neighbor had never complained.

I've also read people complain about a musty smell inside the machine and I've never had an issue like that.  I also make sure to take care of my machine.  After I am done using it I let it cool off a bit (the time it take for me to put the clothing away).  I take a piece of napkin and make sure to dry out the water that stays between the drum and rubber.  After I am done with that, I do not close the machine but leave it open for a couple of hours.  I also remove the detergent dispenser, rinse it, and leave it open as well so it dries out.

I have to admit that I have never cleaned out the inside of the machine for lint...but I plan to have my husband do it soon.  I printed the instructions that I got from someone who reviewed the machine here.  But even with all this time using it and not cleaning it properly, I really haven't had an issue.  I bet when I clean it out my drying time is going to take even less.

In conclusion, if you live in an apartment where regular laundry hook-up is not available and are tired of going to public laundry mats or sharing machines, you should really consider buying this washer/dryer unit.  But do so with the idea that it does not work the same as a regular dryer and therefore you need to adjust the way you do laundry.....but the advantages of having your own machine in the apartment far outweighs any negatives this machine may have. I wish I could hug whoever invented this machine :)

reviewText:
If you are having trouble with the fit, this might help...

I purchased this item and ended up returning it because I thought it was incompatible with my refrigerator. The locking mechanism on this filter was different than the one I was replacing. So I bought the OEM version, and what do you know, it came with the same "wrong" locking mechanism on it.

Took some googling but I finally figured out what happened.

The manual for my refrigerator (RF266AEBP) calls for a #DA29-00003B. That has been discontinued, and the new replacement is #DA29-00003G. Somewhere in there the locking mechanism was also redesigned.

Apparently if you have a filter in place with the old locking design, it will release the filter before the locking collar is turned all the way. This blocks the new filter with the new design from going into the collar. So you have to put the old filter back in and turn it all the way to the left, past where the filter releases. Once you have done that, the new filter with the new locking design will just slip in, with no issues.

I found this info on Samsung's website and wanted to post it here in case people are having issues with getting the new style filter to fit.

reviewText: I have had my refrigerator for 4 years now and have always replaced the filter with the recommended OEM filters religiously every 6 months.  When I looked to replace the OEM filter this time it was unavailable from amazon but a quick search led me to this compatible filter.  It was a little cheaper and I decided to give it a try.  Upon 1st use after the initial installation the water dispenser spitted and sputtered and sprayed black water which had never happened before with the OEM filters.  I decided to run at least two gallons thru the new filter before attempting to drink it.  The first taste was a disgusting weird plastic taste so I ran another 2 gallons of water thru the filter in attempt to further clean it.  I have now had this filter installed for a week and it still has a horrible taste of plastic or something. I have ran multiple gallons of water thru the filter and completely emptied the ice box two times and the awful taste is still there.  I am extremely disappointed in this filter and plan on purchasing an OEM filter to replace it as soon as it is it available again.

reviewText:
When looking to replace my washing machine, I started out looking at the traditional top loaders.  The cheap ones were around $300 and more standard quality ones around $400, and I saw some that were another $100 or more beyond that.  When the salesman asked me about front load washers, I didn't give it much thought because they tend to be more pricey and out of my range.  In fact, the first one he showed me was about $700.  When I asked him what the cheaper ones cost, he showed me the Frigidaire FTF2140FS for $550, and seeing as they had $100 off deal, it was only slightly more than the standard top loader.  So I took him up on the deal, ultimately paying $475 after tax, and so far I'm very happy with its performance.

I usually have two or three people who rent rooms in my house.  The old washer took 40 gallons of water per load.  The Frigidaire takes just 15 gallons!  So right off the bat I'm going to be saving a lot on my water bill, as well as a few bucks on electricity as this washer takes less of that to operate, too.

The controls are pretty simple to figure out.  Ten cycles, four temperature settings, you can set how fast you want the basket to spin, and there's a few extra options, too.  Shut the door, hit start, and let it go.  Just be sure to remember that you must use HE (High Efficiency) detergent.

The wash is very quiet and the spin dry sucks the water right out of the clothes.  I can dry my stuff in half the time!

I did encounter one problem twice.  When washing my bed sheets and blankets, I heard a beeping noise.  The washer was stopped and I thought it was done, but it turns out my sheets were still soaked.  I looked at the manual and apparently if the load in the washer basket becomes uneven, it won't spin (that's why it was beeping).  So I just pushed the blankets a few times, shut the door, and on the cycle dial there's an option for Final Spin, which is just that, the final spin dry.  So I selected that, hit start, and it worked both times.  I haven't had that problem with any other load of laundry before or after that.

This was my first foray into the world of front load washers and so far I'm very pleased with the results!  It's quiet, efficient, my clothes are clean and smell fresh, and most important of all to me, it isn't tearing my clothes like my old washer was.  And for the price I got it at, it's a great value!

reviewText:
I purchased this FACTORY Kenmore Sears Kirkland Roper Dryer Kit by OEM FSP WHIRLPOOL for my personal use. I have a gas dryer that came with the house I purchased a year and a half ago. It was making all sorts of funny sounds for the last few months. Squealing and buzzing specifically. Funds are limited for us due to my wife being disabled. I didn't want to hire a repairman and pay hundreds of dollars. So I researched the problem. In the end I discovered that the squealing nose was the belt and the buzzing sound were from failing solenoids. So I ordered this product and picked up the solenoids from a local parts store. I watched some DIY videos online and fixed the dryer myself. It took a bit of effort on my part but I did it. This dryer kit was integral to returning our dryer to functional use. I was seriously impressed by the cost. It reflects a more than fair value of the product. And when you consider that a dryer repair man visit usually runs between $300 and $400 where I live that cost is a pittance. It came with instructions and diagrams which were helpful to me during the repair process. The parts are high quality. They weren't hard to install at all. And after all was said and done. After this kit was installed and the solenoids were replaced I now have an amazingly quiet dryer that dries clothes efficiently and completely. I highly recommend this product to other people. My wife and I are so glad that we decided to do this ourselves and we saved so much money doing it!

I paid full price for this item. It was not obtained through a promotion.

reviewText:
Have only had this machine for two weeks and cannot speak to its performance over time. Bought it locally.
It does make some pretty funny noises (sometimes sounds like a plane taking off among other sounds; Whirlpool says they're normal). Adjustments must be made, if you've never had a high efficiency (HE) washer before.

For example, unless you turn it off, the timer comes on and makes a sound every time you open the lid. Specific labels (such as "normal," "delicate," or "bulky" set the amount of time for the wash as well as the speed of spins, temperature, etc.. You can change some of the things (temp., spin, etc) but not the time (unless you set it on a different label).

For example, it requires high efficiency laundry detergent. If you use a natural laundry liquid such as those from a health food store, it should say on the package how to adjust for a high efficiency machine if it can be used in one (Whirlpool, who makes this machine, said it was okay to use the natural laundry liquid if it had instructions for use in high efficiency machines; okay to put the natural liquid in the laundry detergent slot, and said it will not damage the tubes that carry the liquid into the washer). I read somewhere that some things can clog this tube if not appropriate for an HE machine.

For example, laundry boosters such as Oxyclean or Borax must be placed in the appropriate amounts on the bottom of the wash basket before placing clothes in the machine (according to the Whirlpool rep.).

High efficiency machines use lots less water than the old machines. Sometimes you have to select "extra rinse" so that all soap is out of an item (such as an item that is stiff with soap residue or other things).

Will let you know in a few months how it goes.

reviewText:
Decided on this model because I want to place the remote sensor in the crawl space. What was first put in place, it read 68 degrees F and 53% relative humidity. Over the three months the temp registered close to the outdoor temperature and the humidity monitor remain 50 to 55%. Just before Thanksgiving the humidity monitor was 99% when I checked it in the morning. An inspection of the crawl space revealed a leak in the hot water line. Once the hot water was turned off, the repair was simple and took about an half hour. It took about 4 days venting the crawl space before the humidity was back down to the mid-50s. Is this unit alerted us to the problem thus preventing extensive damage. That was a good buy for us and I would recommend it for anyone that wishes to or needs to monitor relative humidity in a hard to get at area.
Better yet would be a unit with at least two remotes and an alarm. The alarm could be set so that immediately you would know when something has gone wrong and the second remote could be placed anywhere. In my case my crawl space has two sections because of an add-on to the house. That's where the second remote would be put.
I plan to shop for a unit like that with an alarm and 2 or more remotes. If I cannot find one at a reasonable price then I will buy another one of these for the other crawl space section.
I rated 4 stars vice 5, not because of its performance, but because the readout should be lit so that it can be read easily from anywhere in the room.

reviewText:
I haven't had the opportunity to use this filter yet, but it looks like a nice item for my airbrush. It looks to be a quality made product. Nice and simple hookup to my airbrush. Couldn't ask for anything more !!

OK, after trying to put two of these filters to work, I see I could ask for more !! Both filters fell apart on me. The first filter had 40lbs of air pressure behind it, and it failed immediately when the air was applied. The second filter did the same thing with 30lbs of air pressure. The two filters I received are NO GOOD !! It looks like the clear plastic housing is simply glued to the silver colored (probably plastic) housing. Possibly the glue softened the plastic housings causing the failures ?? I hope to get a complete refund on these. I will edit this feedback accordingly to let it be known if I get a refund or not.

My opinion, THESE ARE NOT WORTH SPENDING YOUR MONEY ON !!

reviewText:
VERY NICE PIECE OF EQUIPMENT FOR THE KITCHEN.  AND I'D GIVE IT 5 STARS EXCEPT THE INSTALLATION IS A BIT OF A NUISANCE.  THE MOUNTS ARE SO DIFFICULT TO GET TO WHEN INSTALLING THAT I THOUGHT IT WAS A JOKE AT FIRST.  i WOUND UP PUTTING TWO STRIPS OF WOOD ON THE SIDES OF THE EXISTING CABINETS TO ALLOW ME TO SUPPORT THIS AS THE 4 SCREWS THAT ARE DESIGNED TO HOLD IT IN PLACE ARE COMPLETELY INADEQUATE.  ONCE INSTALLED, THE UNIT FUNCTIONS PERFECTLY.  I DID FIND ONE SMALL PLASTIC CLIP IN THE BOTTOM OF THE DRAWER THAT LOCKS ONE OF THE PLATE BRACKETS IN THE UPRIGHT POSITION WHEN NEEDED.  IT WAS BROKEN OFF.  I CONTACTED THE MFG WHO IS SENDING ME A REPLACEMENT.  VERY EASY TO USE.  VERY EFFICIENT USING MUCH LESS WATER THAN CONVENTIONAL DESIGNED D/W'S.  MUCH EASIER TO LOAD/UNLOAD AS IT SITS HIGHER THEN THE BACK LOWER DRAWER ON CONVENTIONAL UNITS.  THERE IS EVEN A RED LIGHT INSIDE ON THE HANDLE OF THE JET DRY DISPENSER TO LET YOU KNOW YOU NEED TO REFILL WITH MORE.  VERY HIGH END UNIT.

I wanted to add this after 6 month use:  Absolutely the best.  We discovered that the unit actually steams the dishes after loading even if you don't turn it right on immediately thus softening the debris and keeping it soft until the cycle is started.  We found this out by accident when after loading and forgetting to turn it on the same day we found the dishes had moisture and the debris could actually be washed off in the sink under water.  Just a real well thought out piece of kitchen equipment all the way.

reviewText: Bought this filter after reading the glowing reviews. Two negatives stuck out in the reviews that I wanted to see and test for myself - the fact that this filter is only certified for NSF/ANSI Standard 42 (removal of chlorine taste and odor only) and not Standard 53; cysts, other contaminants) AND the fact that it produced loud noises and slow flow when installed. So figured I'd go ahead and spend the $25 knowing that if they turned out true that I wouldnt buy again and would go back to the more expensive OEM filter. So I got the filter today and after initial install including priming as suggested on the box (i.e. fill with a bit of tap water before installing) and flushing the thing whistled like a steam engine and flow rate was low. I new this was an air leak issue so I simply used vaseline to coat the rubber o-rings on the nipple of the filter adapter at the back of the fridge and then screwed back on and continued to flush and it worked. Nonetheless, already one unnecessary step required so strike 1. To verify the NSF/ANSI claims I simply checked the included spec and sure enough the literature claimed only ANSI/NSF standard 42 approved. Strike 2! I'll use the filter for a few months but definitely not worth the $15 saved considering the wasted time and lack of health standard these things are manufactured to. Only reason I gave it 2 stars is because it works.

reviewText:
If you are single and live in an apartment without space for your own washer and dryer, this is the way to go. It works great to get your clothes clean, and with a little extra labor it is well worth it. I did a little math, it cost me about $10 to $15 a week at the laundromat, that is $50 to $60 a month. My total water bill is $12.00 a month. Do a load with this every other day or so and save at least $40 a month. That adds up to $480.00 a year. That's not including the gas you burn driving there.

Things I've been able to wash with Wonderwash: a bunch of various whites, socks, underwear, a couple of T-shirts; a pair of jeans with a sweatshirt, 2 unlined curtain panels, and even a winged back chair cover.

reviewText:
My wife complained that our electric dryer was taking longer and longer to dry clothes. I ordered this Gardus LintEater system from Amazon after pricing it at my local blue & white big home improvement store where it was almost $10 more.

I used a plug-in drill at first, but the chuck wasn't big enough. My B&D 18v Firestorm drill worked fine. Used a small piece of electrical tape on each extension as I added them, per instructions, but had no problems, just follow the directions and don't reverse the drill or you will unscrew the extension.

My dryer duct goes up from the dryer to the attic and through the roof. After unplugging the dryer, I cleaned the lint trap with the included curved brush, removed the back of the dryer, removed the flex attachment to the duct, brushed out the larger pieces of lint and vaccuumed out the rest.

The LintEater got nearly two quarts of loose lint out of the approximately 10 feet of my vertical duct and was easy to use. My real problem was just above the point where the duct ended and roof vent. Lint had accumulated in this gap and rains had hardened it and eventually blocked the vent. I pulled another quart or two of hardened lint out of this gap and re-opened the vent. I used the vac per instructions to make sure the duct was clear.

I'll be loaning or renting the kit to friends. The LintEater is not made to last forever, but I'm sure I saved $100 doing this myself and with care, it could be used every year for 20-30 years. What more do you want for $26?

reviewText:
Got this for my Maytag QuietSeries 300 dishwasher with a broken handle.  Packaging and delivery from Amazon was superb, and product was a genuine Whirlpool part.  My dismay is with Maytag - the exact part I needed for my particular model did not include the handle, so they recommended this part- which fits, but not exactly.  The handle is a bit too big for the opening, and will only close fully with some added pressure.  I discovered this after installation - all the buttons worked except "start."  After initial panic that I did not install it correctly, I realized that the handle was not completely closed, preventing the dishwasher from starting.  After applying some pressure to the handle to fully close it, it started right up.

For DIYers, the installation was simple.  The biggest challenge for me was removing the electrical connectors from the latch assembly. The connectors were very tight and would not come apart easily for me, and I was afraid of applying too much pressure with the pliers because the connectors are cheap plastic.  I ended up breaking off the old connector while leaving the dishwasher connector intact.

reviewText:
We bought this same refrigerator about five years ago.  Since then we have had repeated problems with it.  The doors had to be replaced once.  The ice maker was replaced four times, one time when it started breaking up and mixing shards of plastic with the ice.  We could have been killed.
This last time the ice maker flooded the kitchen within two hours.  We called GE for repairs and the repairman came several days later.  He installed a new ice maker and said everthing was fine.  He turned on the water and said "All set to go."  When we checked a few hours later, there was no ice, the kitchen was flooding, and sheets of water were coming down from the ceiling in our rec room.  The GE repairman never turned the valve of the water line on completely, so it leaked far far worse than it ever had before.  Knowing to turn water valves completely should be Repair 101.  I called GE back at 3:15; the operator said she would e-mail an immediate request to the repair people in our area.  A taped message contacted us some five days later.  By that time we had bought a new, very different brand.
I can understand a product having problems.  But when the factory authorized repairmen make the problems far, far worse, that manufacturer should be put out of business.

reviewText: Was going to give it four stars since the only way I have to test filtering is anecdotally by tasting it and judging water flow to the GE OEM.  But, I tasted it against my tap water and it tasted noticeably better just like the OEM one.  And I don't recall the original filter tasting any better than this filter.  The filter's papers say "material & components tested and certified by WQA Gold Seal against NSF/ANSI Standard 42 and Standard 53 for the reduction of substances listed below according to Standard 42 and Standard 53."  Cantamination Reduction for Chlorine Taste and Odor had average reduction of 97.1% (NSF Reduction Requirements say greater than or equal to 50%.  Nominal particulate Class I, greater than or equal to .5 to less than 1.0 micrometer average % reduction was 99.8%  with NSF reduction requirements of greater than 85%.  So if you believe their numbers, it passes no problem.  Anecdotally, my water flow seems to be about the same as the OEM when it was new.  My refrigerator is a GE Purifier Performance (older, "Water by Culligan") and water here in Indianapolis, IN is decent (comparatively).  I am wondering where the person who gave this filter bad marks for taste lives, as it definitely is fine for here.  I also wonder if the person who said the filter doesn't fit their refrigerator understands that the original filter was modified to work better (model changed) and required an adapter for some refrigerators (newer refrigerators don't need it).  It is easy to see if this is the case simply by unscrewing your original filter and  looking to see if the new filter's connection looks the same.  The filter was EASY to install, with absolutely no difference with the one I took off.  Didn't need to mark anything or make things complicated. But again, my refrigerator is a GE Purifier Performance, and perhaps other models are different.  The filter is also supposed to have water pressure between 30 and 80 (can be used at higher pressure with a pressure limit valve, if that's the only way you have to limit the pressure), so perhaps people have problems arising from that?  Water was also clear from the get-go with no black filter bits and clear water, so I dunno.  All I know is, I was a bit leery of buying a 'knock off', but having taken the chance, I am currently more than satisfied.  Barring any unforeseen problems/changes I will definitely purchase again, probably the three pack instead of just one.

reviewText: AQUACREST Samsung DA29-00003G Aqua-Pure Plus Water Filter Replacement is advertised as a replacement for Samsung DA29-00003G Aqua-Pure Plus Refrigerator Water Filter, 1-Pack. Considering the difference in price, this would be a miracle if true. However, my experience with this product resulted in some minor appliance repair. The filter leaked, did not allow water to pass through the fridge, and built up a massive amount of pressure. When I tried to remove the filter, the pressurized water went everywhere. This filter is not as advertised and is not a reliable replacement for Samsung DA29-00003G Aqua-Pure Plus Refrigerator Water Filter, 1-Pack. Do yourself a favor and skip the fools errand of trying to save money, and go with the OEM instead.

reviewText:
This little thermometer suits my needs, and having the humidity on such a simple device is a treat!  It is easy to read, and not complicated in any way.  The easy-to-open battery compartment is on the back of the thermometer.  It can be opened with a coin (I used a dime) by a half-turn.  Insert the battery that came with the thermometer, then use the dime to twist the battery cover on.  Also on the back, you can choose to use the kick-out stand so the thermometer rests at an angle.  Or you can choose not to use it, because the thermometer is almost a half-inch wide and stands on its own.  Your choice!

There are 3 red buttons on the front of the thermometer.  The bottom button switches between F and C.  The middle one will display Max and Min temps.  The top one clears it all out.  One simple touch of a button; doesn't get any better than that!

Easy peasy!  If you want a thermometer to work quickly and easily right out of the box, this is the one for you.

reviewText: I have an electric cooking range from Kenmore. After about 3.5 years of use, both the front elements have decided to stay on even after you turn them off. When I called the company to ask for hep, they told me that all their products are made in China and therefore they are not liable. As an attorney I tried to explain to them that since they are putting their name on the product and benefitting from its sales, they are liable for the injury this product may cause. However, they seemed delusional and stuck in their completely illegal ideas. No one ever called me back, even after several attempts. And the company basically washed its hands of the the product and the problem all together. I am never buying ANYTHING from these people again. As their lovely ratings for their cooking ranges already show, they have neither customer support, nor technology backing their brand.

reviewText:
Over the years I've purchased the official GE filter for my fridge.  I just got tired of spending $50 for the filter so I started looking around.  I ended up tracking down this filter.  It was half the cost of the GE filter and fits my fridge perfectly.  Install is exactly the same as the GE model so nothing is complicated through the process.

One thing that I noticed is in the instructions it says to not tighten it too much.  This is a little misleading since you have to wrench it into place pretty hard.  You know it's in place when you hear water jetting into it.  So don't be afraid to give it a firm twist.

When this one is used up I'll definitely be buying another one.

reviewText: Alan Gregerman believes that innovation comes from viewing problems from different and fresh perspectives.  In The Necessity of Strangers, he tells short, simple stories to help free us from current, legacy views of the problem at hand.  He then provides a toolbox containing practical exercises - to stretch our thinking, to collaborate more effectively, to find strangers - all focused on getting us to view problems from different perspectives.  The Necessity of Strangers is a quick read that I keep on the desk as a constant reminder to seek novel views from new sources.  Like matter to anti-matter, The Necessity of Strangers is the perfect and completing complement to his earlier Surrounded by Geniuses.  Gil Miller, Chief Technology Officer, Noblis

reviewText:
Well, it may be better than OK, but the jury is out, as I just received it last night and just started it about 2 hours ago. I did find a couple of issues, however.

First, it arrived with the catch tray for the evaporation from the cooling coil misallocated and broken, with a 1 inch hole in it. However, the pieces were in the bottom of the unit, so I used RTV Silicone gasket sealer, along with the broken pieces, to fix it. I also called Amazon to report it, and as I was willing to fix it and keep it, Amazon gave me an $87.50 refund for the damage! Go Amazon!

The next issue was the door, which is made similar to most refer units, so that one can remove the hinges, move them to the other side, & have the door open in the reverse direction. But, the makers obviously didn't think anyone would actually do this, as it is necessary to turn the door upside down when doing this to get the holes in the door on the same side as the hinges. This would be no big deal, were it not for the fact that they have glued they're logo onto the front of the door's glass, and now that I reversed the door, that logo is upside down, and at the top of the door, where it's very obvious. I'll attempt to remove it and glue it back on right side up, but not until I'm sure the unit is sound and won't need to be returned. Also, the door has a recessed handle for opening that is supposed to be on the top edge of the door. Reversing the door has put this handle at the bottom. Were I using this sitting on the floor, that would mean the handle is all the way down at the bottom near the floor! Fortunately, for me, I had planned to use this on a shelf, and that puts the handle at about waist height. So, in my situation, it wasn't a problem. For other folks, wishing to place the unit on the floor and needing to reverse the door, it will be a small problem. However, were it eliminated all together, one cold still easily open the door by simply grabbing its edge. The handle is mostly a silly gimmicky afterthought, in the first place.

Perhaps my biggest concern, though, is a strange, almost gurgling noise that the unit makes while running. It has become a bit less pronounced since it's been running, and may eventually fade away. Or, it may be indicative of a serious problem that could see the compressor, or the motor, failing in a short time. So, I may have an update to this review in future. Stay tuned!

reviewText: I live in Colorado Springs where the winter humidity is frequently down in the teens. After purchasing six of these registers with filters, I was able to increase and maintain my home's humidity by about an additional 10-15%. However, during cold periods where outside temps stayed below freezing, I had to refill these registers about 3 times a day. I don't know about you, but all of my floor registers are not easily accessible and running around the house 3 times daily to refill the registers can become burdensome. The bottom line is that they work and have saved me the expense of a whole-house humidifier. Be aware also that the wick filters are required for the registers to work correctly, and each filter works about 90 days.

reviewText:
I live in an apartment complex where washer/dryer hookups are non-existent and you have to use their laundromat facilities; well, they recently raised the prices and it costs $6.75 to do one load of laundry from start to finish.  Thats highway robbery and doing laundry every month was costing me an arm and a leg; I decided getting a portable washer was the way to go and that itd pay for itself in the matter of months.  After doing a ton of research and watching a bunch of Youtube videos, I settled on this unit and Im so glad I did.  Yes, this sort of washing machine requires more work and babysitting than your traditional large washing machines but Im more than willing to put the effort in if it means not having to go to the laundromat and being able to do laundry in the comfort of my own home.  I truly love this machine and if it were to stop working on me Id buy a replacement without batting an eyelash.  Below is my start to finish routine that Ive found works best for me (the instructions that come with the machine aren't great at all); this may seem like a lot but once you get the routine down its not a big deal at all:

1. Hook all hoses up and start filling the machine with water (DO NOT fill past the lint catcher thats in the machine or you're going to have a big puddle on your floor).
2. Add a SMALL amount of detergent; Im talking like half of a teaspoons worth.  This machine produces a lot of suds and if you add too much detergent you're going to have to do a ton of rinse cycles.  I also add my scent boosting product at this time.
3. After adding the detergent I turn on the wash cycle just for 15-20 seconds to get the detergent to mix well with the water.
4. Add clothing.  This machine can hold a decent amount; I can wash 2-3 pairs of leggings and 2-3 shirts/blouses per load (depending on weight); I also wash my towels in this machine without a problem (I do one large towel and one hand towel per load).
5. Once the water is filled to the level of the lint catcher I put in the separate lint catcher that I bought here from Amazon (link below) because the built in lint catcher in this machine is totally and completely worthless.  The separate lint catcher floats on top of the water and catches a ton of lint (I have 2 cats, so this is a necessity).  http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00NR295FY?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00
6. Close the lid and set the timer (I usually do 5-12 minutes; all depends on how soiled the items are).
7. Once the wash cycle is done I turn the dial to drain and start loading the items into the spin dryer (I usually fit half a load at a time into the spin dryer) and spin them out for about a minute while the machine is draining the water; once the spin cycle is done I put the items back into the washer for the rinse cycle.
8. Start filling the washer with water again and add fabric softener.
9. Once the water is at the proper level I put the lint catcher back in and turn the washer on for another 5-12 minutes for the rinse cycle.
10. When the rinse cycle ends, turn the dial to drain and transfer the items to the spin dryer and spin dry for about 2 minutes or until water stops draining from the hose.
11. Take items out and hang up to dry fully; the spin dryer on this machine does such a great job of removing water that most items dry completely in 2-3 hours.
12. When Im done doing all laundry I lift the machine up onto my kitchen counter and turn the dial to drain to get all of the water out of the machine (you need gravity for this); the machine is really lightweight, so its not difficult to lift it.
13. Once all water is drained I use a paper towel to dry the inside of the washing & spin dryer tubs; then I leave the lids to both sections open overnight to make sure the machine gets to air dry properly.

Hope this information helps you!!

Our Kenmore Elite washer stopped working (of course right at Christmas)it wouldnt drain or even complete a spin cycle with an F2 error code which after investigating further, through different online help site.. one in peticular Fixya.com. we found out it was the pump.
My husband ended up taking the washer apart after getting direction from the website. Not too difficult but know that you MUST have plenty of pans narrow enough to fit under the washer to catch the water expect some mess of rushing water left over inside the washer. After removing the old pump and draining the water out we found an old shoulder pad lodged inside the pump along with staggnet water which smelled like sulfur. With the debris getting stuck in pump jammed it and broke off the propeller. After receiving the new pump it was VERY simple to install and even works BETTER and quieter then the original pump half the cost of ordering it through other websites!! Thank you AMAZON for making this affordable along with your timely ship, you saved us a boat load of $$$. You won't be dissapointed well worth the $!!

reviewText:
OK, I admit, most of the problems I had installing this unit were my fault.  I bought it not thinking that it is intended to be a REPLACEMENT unit, not an original installation.  As a REPLACEMENT, i.e., swapping out a defective unit for this one, you will have all the extra parts (ice bucket, etc.) that I had to get separately.  If that is your intent you will probably not have any problem with the installation.  I originally intended to return this one but decided to face the challenge and now I'm glad I did.  Now that it's in it works like a champ!  After increasing the water flow the ice cubes are coming out beautifully, and the unit is much faster than I expected.  Filled my fabricated ice bucket that I purchased at the 99 Cent store in a day and a half.  The only complaint I have is that the instructions are woefully inadequate.  Also, I think the supplier should point out that the unit is intended for replacement, not original installation.  Other than that I am very happy with it.

One more thought.  Some previous reviews complained that some of the rear cubes were not ejecting properly and jamming up the unit.  I experienced that as well until I increased the water flow to what I think is about max.  No problem after that.

Would highly recommend it for as a replacement.
