reviewText:
I've had Bosch appliances for years, and used to have a very strong belief in their product line.  If you purchased Bosch goods 10 or so years back, then you probably own a true, German made item.  From my understanding, Bosch has since created a manufacturing plant here in the States, where there just isn't the same commitment to excellence in standards. It also seems that the tolerance of mediocrity is greater here in the US, as consumers seem to have been conditioned to accept/expect less and less over time.

This unit does boast tremendous capacity/storage, and is exceptionally well lit. It really does seem to keep foods, such as fruits and vegetables, more viable over time. The freezer is also commodious, though not quite so as the refrigerator itself.

I've owned this model for 3 years now, as of this writing.
Some problems are/were:
-After delivery, it became evident that the gasket around the freezer door was not properly attached and had to be serviced, albeit easily, though there was a great deal of condensation for a while along the freezer door, before the repair.
-The ice maker seems to make ice only when it is good and ready, and sometimes days will elapse before ice is available.
-The option of crushed ice vs regular cubes - it seems impossible to distinguish between the two, though no big deal.
-The water dispenser is super-slow and takes a while to fill even small glasses.
-A few months ago, there was a loud, vibrating sound that came on and off again, as if one of the fans were off kilter. I was going to call for service, but the sound stopped. I can't detect any difference in operation since the sound stopped, but fear that the fan, or its motor, just eventually blew out.
-The quality of the stainless steel is not the utmost, though decent enough, but just not for the money.

I would proceed with some caution when considering making this purchase, and keep your fingers crossed (which is really no way to go forward).
It may just be that there are some good and bad units of its type out there. I had looked at so many refrigerators prior to the purchase of this one and found that (possibly) for the money, it was about the best. It has the name, but unfortunately, that somehow means less nowadays...

reviewText: Waterdrop Refrigerator Water Filter Replacement for Samsung Aqua-Pure Plus DA29-00003G, DA29-00003F, DA29-00003B, DA29-00003A, HAFCU1, 2 Pack Affordable alternative vs. O.E.M., no problems installing or having leaks. Now, being that said I have no hesitation thinking about running the used filter 2 to 3 times over it's rated use and will just replace it when the fridge says to. It's a no brainer! The only thing I'd note for buyers would be to turn off your water supply to fridge, put a towel under the filter to catch/soak up the water when removing the old filter. Take note of the locking thread position when taking out the old filter as you'll want to insert the new filter with the locking thread in the same position as when you pulled out the old filter, before twisting the filter on.

reviewText: I bought the set three years ago. I have never been able to balance the washer. It lurches around like a drunken sailor whewn on spin cycle no matter how big or small the load is. It slides across the floor, crashes into the washer, vibrates/wobbles from the top. I bought anti vibration pads. No change. I spent a $150 on a plumber to try to level it and no matter what he did it would read level with the leveling tool but would still go off across the room on spin. He suggested as a last resort that I buy a heavy duty rubber floor mat thinking that perhaps the tile floor was too slick so I spent another $40 bucks on one of those. No change. At this point both my tenant and I have concluded that the machine was built wrong and I'll have to trash it and get a new one. I'll never buy another amana or front loader again.

reviewText: My old in wall oven died.  I had done a lot of research to see which option I liked best, but the bottom line was that I wanted to get one that fit into the existing hole in my cabinetry.  This oven fit perfectly.  I did not buy from Amazon, as I know someone with an appliance store, and they recommended this oven as a reliable option for the price.  I saw all of the complaints on Amazon, so I voiced my concerns to my friend.  The previous oven in my kitchen was a Tappan, I think from the 70s, and it actually had the same issue with the left hinge on the door.  My friend explained that Frigidaire actually bought Tappan and had been using that same hinge when they first started building this model.  The hinge had a flaw where it bolted inside of the oven housing, and when it would move to open the door, it would wear out over time.  Due to the amount of failures and issues they had with this design, the reworked the hinge so that it is fixed to the outside of the main oven housing and hinges at that point.  Let me tell you, I tried it in the store, and it moved SMOOTH.  It is now installed, I've used it for about a month and a half and it still has a great feel.  I'm not crazy about the buttons, they feel like they might not be the best buttons to last a long time, but we'll see.  They do feel nice to press, and they are responsive, so if they do hold up, I have no complaints.  I have the black oven and I love the finish of the entire thing.  So, before you go and just decide NOT to buy this due to all of the negative reviews, be sure to do your homework, contact the actual seller and find out what the hinge is like.  I will try to post a picture of what my hinges look like, so that you know what to look and ask for.

The product is extremely lightweight for its size, and is made almost entirely out of plastic. I hope it holds up well.

So far I have used it for 10 loads. It gets most of the water out of the clothes even after the spin cycle of my standard washing machine. The clothes will come out damp. How damp? Cool to the touch damp. Clothes would be unpleasant to wear on a cold day, but a perfect treat on a hot summer's day. Cotton socks would definitely be too damp to wear after a single spin. If you iron your clothes, shirts and slacks will be totally dry after a regular ironing.

I have only ran one load of cotton socks. Socks retain lots of the moisture. Next time I will try running the spin cycle  two or three times to see how much comes out of the socks. I have yet to run jeans through, but I anticipate that they will require multiple cycles.

Running a smaller load is more effective. I usually run them twice or thrice to get all of the water out that I can. Each cycle takes 5 minutes.

reviewText: Have owned the standard Fisher Paykel version of this double oven for 22 months.  I am assuming electronics and elements are the same as the external controls are virtually identical.  Summary: a $4000 oven that needs the second oven as one oven is regularly inoperative or worse yet inconsistently inoperative.  Issues are twofold.  Service via Sears maintenance agreement is not good.  Have had 5 service calls.  Has always taken at least 7 working days to have someone show up.  They diagnose the problem.  They do not carry the parts and have to order.  Typical resolution from placing service call to having problem fixed is 3 calendar weeks.  That is why you need the second oven while you wait for the other to be fixed.  Problems 1) burnt convection element.  2)Bad control board.  3)Intermittent door switch and hence intermittent heating element operation. 4) Buzzing of entire oven.  This was incredibly annoying.  First tech said - poor installation.  Second one tightened all the screws on sides and rear of oven and solved the buzzing.  One would expect this to be done at the factory. 5) Oven would pretend to work but would not actually turn on.  Displays were dim.  Had to reset breakers on fuse panel to reset oven controls. Techs also suggested limiting oven self cleaning as this was "suspected" of causing premature element failure.  Bottom line is that while this oven looks good, it appears to have both technical and support problems that make it unsuitable for actually cooking food.

reviewText:
My side-by-side Kenmore got cold intermittently. Evaporator fan does not work. So I narrow down the problem to the defrost controller, the evaporator fan and the defrost thermostat. Since the price at Amazon is so good that I decide to buy all three of them just in case of multiple issues combined. After replacing the controller, the fridge worked well for a week before it has the same problem again. The fan is a culprit in this case...Back to the controller, there are a lot of wires, it is a little complicated to replace it. I follow the instruction and replace wires group by group...
NOTE: In fact, when replacing the fan, I connected it to the wrong wire, so the controller got burned. Using old controller with new fan fix my fridge problem.

reviewText: We ended up getting this unit solely because of the cutout size of the existing cabinetry and this was the best(and most expensive) option we seemed to have. We had a Whirlpool prior to this and the microwave failed years ago, while the oven was still functioning--never get a combination unit for that reason. Installed on 12/27/2012 and ever since then, there are three types of malfunctions in the microwave: 1. There is still :01 secs in the timer left when the microwave reads "Cooking Complete" and beeps. This seems to be the most innocuous, as otherwise the food gets hot. May be a rounding error in the software, but happens very frequently. I will upload a video. 2. In this one, the microwave is on, turntable rotates, counter counts down, but the sound is a low hum of the turntable rotating, but the fan is not on. This is with the regular microwave function, not the convection part. So far, this has happened about 6-7 times. We haven't tested the food, whether it gets hot during those times. I haven't been able to replicate it, as when the technician came, but fortunately, I had videotaped it as it happened again the day before he came. It happens at random times and random settings. 3. This third type has happened about thrice so far, where when time is entered for a full power cook function and upon pressing start, nothing happens. No sound, no countdown, no turntable rotating, but the microwave displays "Cooking..." as when it does when everything works correctly. So far no luck with customer service. Will report if anything changes. Updated on 3/5/2013. I have uploaded videos of the above malfunctions at: [...] Updated on 07/25/2013. I notice that there are more people having issues with this unit. Please post your complaint on: [...]. That was the only way I got a prompt response and they eventually replaced the unit, as I had meticulously kept a video log of every event of malfunction. That didn't solve the problems, but they happen less frequently in the new unit. Best advice for people considering this unit is to stay away! Updated on 11/14/13. Our saga continues even with the replacement unit. I wanted to wait and see, even ignoring the timer not counting down to zero before displaying "Cooking Complete" and the fan not turning on or off as it does sometimes. On 11/11, when the microwave did not even turn on, even when the display said "Cooking", it was the last straw. I have complained on their facebook page and will wait to see what action they are going to take. If replacing the unit did not solve the problem, I don't know what will. Obviously, there is a problem with this model. Please stay away. Do not buy this product. If there was a zero star rating, I would give it.

reviewText:
After I moved into my New apt. and had to pay for my water for 1st time I decided to buy a Brand New Washer & Dryer set,at the time I had a little X-tra money and seeing I was going to be on a Fixed Income now that it might be a good idea,washers & Dryers usually last at least 10 years or so and it was an Energy Star,save more money and being a single mom this was good.
 Well things went well for awhile the washer cleaned well and spun so much water out they took no time at all to dry esp. in my New Matching Dryer,then one day I goto check my wash and find my Basement is Flooded & water is Comming out the Front Door of this Washer,so I run to shut it off,and clean up the water that I have to Pay for,then we try again and yep it still is leaking out and I end up having to call a Repair Guy,he comes and finds the problem but doesn't carry the needed part but charges me about 60.00,I order the part & Install myself to save a few bucks and it works fine,for awhile anyway...
  A few months latter Again we have the Flow of Water out the Front Door of the washer but no real reason for it,and again I have to clean up water all over the Basement & then it seems to be fine and working. So a few months latter I throw in a load of laundry and when it was done I went to get it,well I can't seem to get the Washer Door Open and Nothing I did would get it to Open it's holding my wash Hostage and I was SO MAD Now What? I looked,I fiddled,I even called my electrician friend to see if he could by pass a switch or something but Nope that didn't work & I just didn't have the money to get the repair guy over again so I left the wash in there,either that or I break the door to get my wash & I allready wasted enough I felt,thank goodness I still had my old washer to use.
  Not long after washer Nightmare I went to dry my wash and the Dryer was making a Horrible noise,so I look around inside mabey it's change or something,Nope,I was afraid to use it,it sounded that Bad. So after going to the laundromat a few times I said this is Stupid,so I went & took the Dryer apart to see if I could see anything,well let me warn you if U ever need to look inside yours be Carfull it's Super Sharp inside,guess they couldn't bother to grind the sharp edges,my son ran & got me a couple Band-Aids & I kept going,I didn't see anything wrong and put it all back together,the noise isn't as bad as before so I'm just gonna keep using it as long as I can unless I find a new affordable set.
 I only used the "Set" for around 2 years & Less before I started having problems with Washer.
  When they say it's Energy Efficient there Not Lying esp. when the washer uses No Electricity when it's Not Plugged In/Working & Great on Water esp. when it's no longer hooked-up!!
  I wish I had the X-Tra 400.00 for extended Warranty but I could've bought a brand new one for that much.

  For Sale Energy King,Queen: Broken Fairly New Washer/Dryer Set,Excellent on Water & Elect. Kidding....

reviewText: My wife and I retired about 9 years ago. We bought a top of the line Maytag front loader set. The washer lasted four years. Called Alabama Power Service the repairman came out looked at it, jiggled the tub and with a sad expression on his face said, "It's worn out...cost more to fix that replace. Asked for recommendations, my mother's Maytag lasted forty years,he recommended a Whirlpool top loader, it lasted four years. Same repairman came out said the Whirlpool was worn out. When I exclaimed this was the product he recommended, he replied none of'em last forever. Both instances he charged us $92 and left. I went to complain to Alabama Power. The lady was sympathetic but the service is contracted out. Of course no recourse...While there I saw this machine and learned it uses water pressure instead of an agitator to clean the clothes. It will cost us $12.30/year for water, 1/3 of the previous machines and it's energy efficient and uses high efficient detergent. I have set and watched it through about 20 loads, it works like a champ. I'll let you know in four years if it lasts. For now we are tickled to death with it.

reviewText:
This is a fantastic washer and all the 1 star reviews seem to come from buyers who did not bother to read the product dimensions (yet spent $2,000 and blame the Beetle they bought for not being able to carry the same amount of luggage like their previous truck managed). Their reviews shouldn't rate this product but their own poor buying decision "process". Embarrassing.
These machines are great when you are in an appartment, want to fit them into your kitchen or simply if you are a single person household.
Or whenever you value quality (as in getting the most gentle yet most perfect cleaning job for your expensive clothes) over quantity (washing 15 pairs of jeans at once won't work here). They use less water, spin the laundry almost closet-dry, are super quite and your fancy clothes last longer.
If you're like me, the stuff you wash in one load sometimes exceeds the value of the washer which puts the high price tag into perspective (and here you get quality and not just a label)....
Think about buying it wisely for another reason: you will have this machine for 20, 30 or even 40 years. My mom has a Miele vacuum that she bought in 1970. I will inherit it. They still make the bags.
If you get one, go European all the way: use it with Persil Megaperls! And like Europeans, get two boxes: One for whites (you can get the cheaper Persil ProClean sold here) and for colors spend a bit more for the Original German stuff https://www.amazon.com/Persil-Megaperls-Color-3-256-Loads/dp/B00HWNH1E6/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1483125213&sr=8-1&keywords=persil+color+megaperls
as it is formulated without color-fading brighteners like most US stuff is. A little goes a long way. Stay away from Tide unless you want black shirts to turn gray after 5 washes.

reviewText: Unit is quiet and efficient.  The clothes are almost dry by the end of spin cycle.  Holds a lot of laundry.  I had a hard time getting the unit so am really glad I like what I finally got.  The third party used to order (Gerhardts) didn't delivery the first shown date and told me it was out of stock.  I cancelled the order and ordered another washer from another dealer.  Low and behold I get a "shipped" notice from Gerhardts.  I had to scramble to cancel the second order so I didn't receive 2 units!!  When I tried to call Gerhardts to see if they had really shipped I was transferred to no less than 5 people before the last one "transferred" me to dead air.  I called back and got someone who was kind enough to find me the "right" person who "was new and not sure how to find my product".  She did find me in the system and let me know that my product had indeed shipped (of course, when I got my notice from the delivery company, the item had not actually "shipped" for two more days).  That put me almost three weeks beyond the first delivery date when I could have had the second washer guaranteed in four days after the order.  I learned a hard lesson (and also found out how many clothes I have) to not order large appliances from Amazon.  I have NEVER had so much problems getting a product from Amazon.

reviewText: After reading the reviews I took a chance and bought one to see for myself. The installation didn't take as long as I thought it would. I had no problems at all. I spray painted it white with spray paint made for plastic. Before installation I did buy a tube of silicone caulking to ensure a tight seal on the interior as well as the exterior of the vent. My exterior is stucco, and I had no problems with any of the supplied hardware drilling into the exterior of the house. I even pre drilled some holes which really wasn't even necessary. After installation was complete, I squeezed a small bead of caulking around any possible gaps to make it air tight. It worked! I let the caulking set, then  took off the top cap of the vent while my wife turned on the dryer inside. The round cap inside the vent flew up in the air and out of the vent. Obviously this meant I had a pretty good seal. The next morning I opened up our laundry room door and noticed a pretty big difference in temperature as compared to the ice box feeling we used to get when going into our laundry room. To us, it is worth the small amount of money to make our laundry room less drafty and so cold like it used to be. I don't know how some people have such a hard time installing something so basic, and complain about having to squeeze out a little caulking from a tube. Don't believe the negativity. if your laundry room is like ours was, buy it. It's not going to heat the room but will definitely help keep it warmer in the cold season and keep the bugs out. We're very happy with the purchase.

reviewText:
I bought this to replace a magnetron in a 2.0 cu ft. GE over the range microwave that would be many hundreds $ to replace and finding an over the range machine w/2 cu. ft. and a 14" turntable is not common. GE would only make good on their 10 year (part only) magnetron warranty if they came to check/verify that it was bad and then another service call to replace it if it was bad + their outrageous price for any ancillary parts found bad ($35 for a $5 diode etc.). Basically $160 - $240 to replace a part I had properly diagnosed as bad. No thanks GE, won't be participating in your thinly disguised service call generation practice called a "part warranty".

The replacement for the orig. (Samsung OM75P(10)ERHN / GE# WB27X10735) mag. called for 10QBP0229 but this 0230 unit says it covers the 0229 w/the stud kit included and this 0230 was less $ than the 0229. The only chance I took was that the stud kit would be missing and if the picture (w/separated male terminals) was correct I would have to separate the connectors out of the one piece female plug on the machine.

As others have said, the picture is wrong and the mag. connector mated the one piece female plug perfectly. If you want to see what the connector will probably actually look like look up the 10QBP0229.

Machine is up and running for under $40 and my time. 1 star deducted for inaccurate product picture. Frankly, if you can't handle the different connector issue you probably shouldn't be fooling around inside the guts of your microwave.

Here are two magnetron reference resources I think are useful
[...]
[...]

and a more general "Notes on the Troubleshooting and Repair of Microwave Ovens"
[...]

reviewText:
So grateful that replacing the drain pump fixed all of our problems because at first I was afraid it was a tub bearing and that is a very difficult repair.

We have a Maytag Performa model # PAVT244AWW that we bought in 2003 and this drain pump worked perfectly.

A couple of years ago, we had to replace the snubber ring; so compared to that this was a snap.

We watched some drain pump videos on YouTube by "appliance parts pros" and the "appliance man" which made everything pretty straightforward.

 Just make sure you clamp your drain pump hoses tight or they will slip off during use.

Our washer was making a horrible grinding noise every time the water was draining from the washer and now everything is better than ever. The washer is so quiet. I think the pump had been going for quite some time.

reviewText:
Around 10 years ago my old GE (around 20 yeras old) has stopped making ice...Never knew exactly what the problem was and I tought it would be too expensive for technical support to repair it. Then I decided to figure out the problem by myself and the dual solenoid valve was the cause. Only the ice maker valve was faulty, the cold water was working fine.
After searching and contacting GE in my country I had only found very expensive replacement parts, so I have stopped my mission to repair the ice maker... Until I found this cheap replacement part and decided to give it a try.
Within a week I had the part in my mail but installing it was not easy. The inlet water connection is not a standard measurement (at least here in Portugal) and had I've searched for an adaptor in every store around here without any success. I've asked a guy to machine an adaptor with his industrial tools. With this new adaptor I was able to connect the watter supply to the valve and within 5 minutes everything was working and the ice maker was working again after several years.

summary: 10 years later my old GE is making ice again
text:
10 years later my old GE is making ice again. Around 10 years ago my old GE (around 20 yeras old) has stopped making ice...Never knew exactly what the problem was and I tought it would be too expensive for technical support to repair it. Then I decided to figure out the problem by myself and the dual solenoid valve was the cause. Only the ice maker valve was faulty, the cold water was working fine.
After searching and contacting GE in my country I had only found very expensive replacement parts, so I have stopped my mission to repair the ice maker... Until I found this cheap replacement part and decided to give it a try.
Within a week I had the part in my mail but installing it was not easy. The inlet water connection is not a standard measurement (at least here in Portugal) and had I've searched for an adaptor in every store around here without any success. I've asked a guy to machine an adaptor with his industrial tools. With this new adaptor I was able to connect the watter supply to the valve and within 5 minutes everything was working and the ice maker was working again after several years.

Even with the problem of the inlet connection measurement not being standard I definitely would recomment this part. It has been working non-stop for some weeks since with the hot weather in the summer the ice maker almost cannot cope with our ice demand :)
label: Positive

reviewText:
Stop your research this is the Oven Range you need. I have had this Double Oven Range for almost 6 months now and it is a joy to use every time.

Pros:

The burners are great at high heat and low heat. The Double burner can be used to boil gallons of water quickly.

The convection oven on the bottom is great for baking cookies and breads, everything has turned out great.

Do not worry about space. Most of the space in a traditional oven is wasted. The double oven allows you to be much more efficient and heat a smaller area on a daily basis. Then you can get them both going for holidays. We cooked a 20 pound turkey and a Spiral Ham at the same time and had room for biscuits and a few other things that were in and out of the oven.

The Slate color, imo, is so much better than Stainless Steel. No fingerprints, no mess, very easy to clean.

Slide out rack. It telescopes out with very little force and stays balanced. This is a nice understated touch that is very helpful in day to day baking.

Not Particular to this Double Oven Range but we were finally able to upgrade to natural gas over electricity and life is so much better as a result. Electric Ranges are the worst.

The cons:

The Timer is really unintuitive. There is no feedback that you set the timer. In the beginning I had to wait 60 secs just to see the minute place move to know that the timer was set properly. It takes some getting used to. I use my cell phone or microwave for timing more often anyways so there are very few times I have to worry about the Timer in this Double Oven Range.

reviewText:
This Panda xpb45 replaced a 3.6 cubic foot American made top-loader that cracked the outer tub during repetitive power failures and started leaking.  The machine was only 4 1/2 years old.  The Panda has surprised us by creating really dirty, opaque, wash water on the first loads we ran.  I thought at first that it was really dirty clothes, but when I washed a load of unworn "clean" shirts from the closet, the wash water was still grey and dirty looking.  After 3 or 4 times through the wash cycle without changing detergents or spot treatments the wash water looks like one would expect,  a little dirty (brownish), but not opaque.

The Panda lets me soak a load overnight if necessary or convenient.  Its' predecessor would automatically drain after 3 or 4 hours in a soak cycle unless it was unplugged from the wall.  I used to pretreat even small bloodstains with hydrogen peroxide to remove them.  With the new machine, unless it is a large bloodstain, it washes out without pre-treatment.

Washing is now a manual process which requires minor thought.  I actually have to remember whether it just finished a wash cycle or a soak cycle.  The spin-rinse tub works surprisingly well, I always liked the spin-rinses that the washers of the 60's had.  I know it is not intended, but a spin-rinse on clothes with hanger dust on them is usually enough to run them through dryer and wear them.  We may save enough money from the shorter time that the electric dryer runs to pay for the washer before it wears out (I hope).

The colored stain in the tub from new clothes washes out after a couple of loads without any visible transference to following loads.

I guess that all I can say is that so far, it works better than Federally mandated designs of automatic washers.

reviewText:
Started working within an hour.  BUT, the instructions are terrible!  It was easy enough to install (5 min).  I reused my wiring harness and removed the clip on the underside of my old icemaker with a pliers so it would hold the plastic piece on wiring harness in the correct spot.  They should leave the clip loose.  It says to "breakout" the plastic piece for the filling tube, but it actually must be cut with a blade.  My old ice maker had a third support bracket and screw that I removed, but the screw is too long for this icemaker - no biggy - still works.  I cannot figure out what the extra white bracket and screw are for!?!  Nothing in the instructions about it.  Cover for the filling tube?  But it doesn't seem to fit my refrigerator's filling tube.  I also had to remove the wire bar and turn it around, nothing in the instructions about that either.  The tray is plastic where my old one was a coated metal and seemed sturdy, but runs about $57.  If this one last as long, it's a good buy, otherwise, it might be better to get the original and have it fit better and not spend a half hour searching for proper instructions which I never found.

text:
Very bad instructions, but easy to install.. Started working within an hour.  BUT, the instructions are terrible!  It was easy enough to install (5 min).  I reused my wiring harness and removed the clip on the underside of my old icemaker with a pliers so it would hold the plastic piece on wiring harness in the correct spot.  They should leave the clip loose.  It says to "breakout" the plastic piece for the filling tube, but it actually must be cut with a blade.  My old ice maker had a third support bracket and screw that I removed, but the screw is too long for this icemaker - no biggy - still works.  I cannot figure out what the extra white bracket and screw are for!?!  Nothing in the instructions about it.  Cover for the filling tube?  But it doesn't seem to fit my refrigerator's filling tube.  I also had to remove the wire bar and turn it around, nothing in the instructions about that either.  The tray is plastic where my old one was a coated metal and seemed sturdy, but runs about $57.  If this one last as long, it's a good buy, otherwise, it might be better to get the original and have it fit better and not spend a half hour searching for proper instructions which I never found.

reviewText:
Badly out of calibration.  Ordered one from Amazon, and it was at the lower end of the calibration range part of the time (read 70% in a regulated 75% RH environment), rest of the time was out of the the range (read <70% RH).  Amazon replaced it without question, but the new unit was even farther outside the range.

These little units are NOT configured so you can adjust the displayed RH as some other units are.

If you need to know what the relative RH is with any degree of precision, these are not the units for you.  Extech seems to think +/- 5% is good enough.  If they made clocks with that level of precision, you could get a clock that could gain or lose one hour and 12 minutes every day and it would be considered accurate..

If you want to maintain your humidor between 65-70% RH.  When it is really 60-65% there will likely be an unpleasant surprise.

And yes, I could just mentally add 5 or 6 or 7 or% to the readout, but that's not why I paid my money.

Avoid this unit if precision is important to you .

reviewText: Perfect fit for our fridge (Whirlpool). The same filters sell for one for $50+ at our local orange or blue big box stores, and that's insane. We've purchased these before and they worked very well so we went with this same manufacturer again. The quality is excellent, the fit is perfect. No water spraying or dripping out from the connection point (something we've had with the expensive "don't buy any other type but these or you'll meet your doom" filters). Just follow the instructions for soaking in water for about 10 minutes before putting into your fridge's filter port and then run the water through until it is clear, which is maybe five or six large 28oz glasses, and you are good to go. (I save the water with the charcoal dust for our houseplants, they really seem to like it.). No problems with air pockets or sputtering, and the reminder stickers they include are helpful. Will definitely buy filters from them again. Water tastes clean with absolutely no chemical or metallic taste and has no scent. Makes our city water taste good!

reviewText:
Zero stars would be more appropriate.

I bought this with the hope that it would help with cleaning up the various stains that come with everyday living.  Not even close. First, the push-button to spray the cleaning solution is cheap and feels like it's going to break and it dispenses only a tiny amount of solution.  To cover a stain about 2 inches by 1 inch, you have to keep pressing the button. It took about 10 pushes to actually "cover" the stain. After waiting the required 2 minutes, you set the device to "scrub". The scrubber bristles are softer than my toothbrush and barely come in contact with the carpet, even when pushing down. The idea is that the scrubbing action will work the cleaning solution into the carpet and loosen up the dirt.  Then you switch to the "vacuum" setting, which is supposed to suck up the dirty cleaning solution. Another great idea with poor execution. After several minutes and lots of pressing the machine into the carpet, not a single water drop was removed. I double-checked the instructions to make sure I didn't miss anything - I didn't. I had followed them to the letter.

So, I tried again. I've tried this device on several different types of stains now and it hasn't done a darn thing.  New stains, old stains, simple spills, light dirt, pet stains, mystery stains, it doesn't matter, this thing is a piece of junk.

reviewText: I've had company since May 2017.  With continuous eating and drinking, my silver is constantly used.  Before long, the basket that came with the Frigidaire automatic dishwasher was filled up but the rest of the dishwasher wasn't.  I don't  "crowd" spoons, forks, etc. and want it spacious so tableware is washed/rinsed well during the different cycles.  I was a little unsure if this GE silverware basket would fit.  The reviews were good so I gave it a try.  Am so glad I did because it fit right in the last row.  With the 2 baskets I can now put spoons and forks, large and small, in their own compartment and have compartments left for serving pieces.  In the end, it saves me time getting it out of the dishwasher and putting everything in the place where they belong.  I recommend this for a busy household.

reviewText:
We often had intermittent te error (actually it's an upside down F)
Yes re-seating the old thermistor plug (unplugging and replugging) would work for months to a year, but sooner or later the error always came back.

I ordered this part last year but never needed to put it in till today when the error finally came back and this time no matter what I did could not get the error to clear like I did before, that is, until I removed the old and inserted the new one I had sitting there on the shelf for the last year, and now it works fine again.

It's very very easy to change this. There was no need to take off the door or front panel or anything else - on my LG Wash Machine Model WM0642H anyway. I just gently tipped the wash machine onto it's side, and can easily access the part right from the bottom as I kneeled to get at it.  Also no tools needed to change the part either, the part is only firmly seated into a rubber holder, and so I only had to gently twist/turn it out of it's rubber holder, and to install just slide the new one into its place, and plug the plug.  This time I gently preened the plugs fittings where it goes into its plug so that the new one will seated tighter....electrically that is.

We shall see how long this lasts, but for $6 total I just ordered yet another spare

reviewText:
Ouch -- pricey filter.  Does the job but soooo expensive.

My suggestion -- buy and replace less frequently.  Next time you replace, put on a pair of safety glasses and take the used filter outside and place on a hard surface.  Strike it hard with a sharp object (like and axe) and break the plastic housing.  Look inside.  I found the charcoal filter clean and the white paper outer liner clean.  Yes, I know we can't visually see chemicals trapped in the charcoal filter but my assertion is that changing ever 6 months in a household with light ice/chilled water use is probably overkill.  Replace yearly vs. every 6 months and cut you cost in half.  Just my suggestion

reviewText: Ok, so I've had this fridge for 1 1/2 months.  It is keeping my 6 11oz Vega protein drinks[in door], 4 18oz ice tea bottles [on inside bottom] and 8 Triple Zero yogurts [4 on mid shelf/4 on top shelf] nice and cold.  I'm the only person in my 18x24 office and it sits in the corner on the other side of U shaped desk.  It does make a noise that is noticeable if you're listening for it but not near as loud as the dehumidifier that sits near it or the Xerox Phaser printer cycling that sits directly behind me.  It easily becomes one of background noises that you don't even notice.  It keeps everything nice and cold (especially my protein drinks and I probably only have it set at 2/3 temp.  Arrived in time and in expected shape (no dings, scratches, dents).  Custodians want to put a tray under it in the event of a leak, but all's been ok so far (sitting on very short pile office carpet).  I'm very glad I made this purchase so I can have my food items as scheduled when my bosses office door is closed, not allowing access to our kitchen (larger fridge).

reviewText:
We have has the Bosch under counter washer and dryer for 10 years.  The motor just burned out on the washer and we are replacing it with another Bosch.

All of the complaints about the smell are due to user ignorance.  We had the same problem.  I tried every method and product to eliminate it and could not.  Then I came across one suggestion on the Internet that worked 100%.  We switched fom liquid Tide HE to powdered Tide HE and within two weeks the smell was completely gone and never returned.

I agree it does not clean as well as a full size machine.....find me one that is better!

reviewText:
I received the 15lb unit today and I am stoked. We live in a small apartment and although we do have coin laundry available, getting quarters is a pain and... I just hate it. I tested this immediately.

I had no trouble at all with the hose, maybe people are using the wrong end? It popped right on and stayed there. It took very little soap to wash the load, which was an impressive amount of clothes. It's obviously much more manual than a traditional washer, you have to move the clothes from one side to the other between spin and wash cycles, but it got the job done in about half an hour.

I did learn my lesson about letting the hose sit on the floor while the machine is on, even though it wasn't in drain cycle. Don't do that! Oops 

Anyway, I'm very excited. No more skipping laundry just because I don't have quarters and no more hauling loads up and down two flights of stairs, and no more waiting for neighbors to come get their clothes. Definitely worth the money!

reviewText:
We bought a new house several weeks ago.  I needed a new refrigerator, washer, dryer, and dishwasher.  The dishwasher was to replace the builder grade GE that came with the house.  As many others did, I researched and read tons of reviews.  I came to the conclusion that there is no such thing as a universally positively reviewed appliance of any kind.

The GE that was in the house before was awful - one batch of dishes after having spaghetti sauce, and the white interior was pink from that point forward.  It was also horribly loud.  We tried not to run it when we would be in the general area.  Even running it at night, I could hear it from the master bedroom.  It was also difficult to load, and did not offer much space.

And then, along came this Kitchenaid.  It is like night and day, and yes I do think you get what you pay for.  I was eligible for an employee discount from Whirlpool, so I did get this model for less than retail, but it is still fairly expensive.  Why do I love it so?  It is substantial - stainless tub, heavy door, satisfyingly substantial.  It has TONS of room in the tub.  I like how easy it is to adjust the top dish rack up and down to accommodate various sizes of pots or dishes.  I also like the utensil rack at the top, and I have used that quite a few times already.  We have found it very easy to load - we have Fiestaware dishes and a variety of pots and pans, including a stock pot and a range of saute pan sizes.  No problem loading any of them so far.  It has many washing/drying options, and I like the inclusion of a 1-hour quick wash for those days when I'm doing a lot of cooking and need to turn dishes around quickly.

But the best thing of all is....how *quiet* it is.  We really cannot tell when it's running.  We have put our hands on it several times just to confirm that the blue indicator light isn't lying to us.

I also read some complaints about steam venting onto cabinets.  There is a small vent on the upper left side of the unit, and a few said that it blew steam onto their cabinets.  I have not seen this happen at all, and I have been watching for it.  No steam, no moisture on the cabinets.

As it has only been a few weeks, I'm still curious to see if we run into any problems.  So far, so good.  If that changes, I will update my review.

reviewText: This Supco RCO410 start capacitor kit solved my "refrigerator not getting cold" problem and at a fraction of the cost of the original replacement unit,(under $15 from Amazon), not to mention the cost if I would have had a repairman come out to my house.  Start by removing the refrigerator electrical cord from the wall and remove the bottom cover where the compressor is located.  Assuming that the cutout relay/capacitor assembly to your compressor is bad (mine had a bad smell), remove and discard the old unit assembly after cutting the power wires to the unit.  Mine were the red and white wires but yours may be different.  In any case, these two cut wires bring the 120 VAC to the red and black wires of the start capacitor unit.  Strip the insulation back on the two cut wires and hook them up to the pre-stripped red and black wires of the start kit capacitor using the provided wire nuts.  It does not matter which of the pre-stripped red & black wires from the capacitor unit are connected to the power wires cut from the old relay, because AC does not care about polarity.  This leaves 3 wires of the start kit capacitor remaining to be hooked up to the compressor.  Make sure that you identify the compressor pins correctly.  There are 3 pins coming out of the compressor.  Two of the pins should have a resistance of around 10 ohms between each other, therefore the remaining pin is the "common".  The remaining 3 wires of the capacitor unit has spade lugs that will easily attach to the compressor pins.  The black wire spade lug of the capacitor goes to the common pin on the compressor.  The measured resistance between the common and the other 2 pins on the compressor should be approximately 6 ohms and 4 ohms respectively.  The white wire spade lug from the capacitor goes to the pin measuring 6 ohms (start winding) and the red wire spade lug goes to the pin measuring 4 ohms (run winding).  Test repair by plugging in the refrigerator to see if the compressor is running. Allow about 15 minutes or so to make sure that the refrigerator is becoming cold, then unplug refrigerator wall plug, secure capacitor assembly such as with a nylon tie strap, install refrigerator back cover, plug in the refrigerator to the wall and slide into place.  There are plenty of do-it-yourself videos on YouTube, but be careful on how the 3 pins on the compressor are wired.  I say this because mine were upside down from what most videos show.  My refrigerator has been running fine to present date (about 2 weeks) with no further problems.  At this point, I would highly recommend the RCO410 capacitor kit.  If it doesn't work for you, all you are out is a little of your time and less than $15 bucks.  Good luck!

reviewText:
I have a Whirlpool wtw6400sw2 that developped the F1 error code.

Good:
- It fixed the error code
- It changed the cycles somehow and seems to be quiter when filling the tub than before
- It wasn't to much trouble to replace, as it's mounted on the top back of the machine. Did require an unusual screwdriver head to remove the old one and put on the new one.

Bad:
- Loads with large items (like lots of towels or a blanket) seem to get themselves made into a ball and bang the machine around (then the machine stops until you untangle the load) a lot more than before. It wasn't perfect before, but I just washed several blankets I use on my bed one at a time and every one of them tangled themselves up into a ball and started thunking the machine around partway through the cycle before the machine shut itself down. I did a max capacity load of towels and there seems to be more of the same thing with those to. I don't remember having this issue before to this extent.

Bottom line - if your only issue is the F2 error code, this is probably worth it. If you have any other issues to fix on the washer it's probably best to buy a new machine.
